question: What library do I use to build a passkey-based smart wallet on Stellar?

golden.answer:
For a new project, use **`smart-account-kit`** with the relevant **OpenZeppelin Stellar smart-account contracts**, then pin and inspect the exact SDK, verifier, signer, context-rule, and policy contract versions you deploy. As of **2026-07-11**, npm showed Smart Account Kit **0.4.2**; OpenZeppelin `stellar-contracts` stable release **v0.7.2** was observed on **2026-06-09**. The kit supports WebAuthn/passkey and delegated or external signer flows plus context rules and policy-based authorization, but exact verifier and policy capabilities belong to the selected releases.

Treat **`passkey-kit`** as the legacy predecessor. Its current README directs greenfield users to Smart Account Kit and explicitly labels the code **unaudited demo material** that should not secure real value. Do not infer that Smart Account Kit itself is SDF-maintained or that every application component inherits an OpenZeppelin audit. A separate G-account or relayer may submit and pay, but authorization remains in the C-account's `__check_auth` path and selected signer/verifier/policy contracts.
keyFacts: Routes greenfield work to smart-account-kit plus the relevant OpenZeppelin Stellar account contracts.
Dates Smart Account Kit 0.4.2 and OpenZeppelin stellar-contracts v0.7.2 to their observed releases.
Preserves Passkey Kit's legacy, unaudited-demo, no-real-value warning.
Ties signer/verifier/context/policy behavior and audit claims to exact selected versions and separates relaying from authorization.
avoid: Do NOT present Passkey Kit as the safe greenfield default or omit its unaudited/no-real-value warning.
Do NOT say both kits expose identical primitives or APIs across versions.
Do NOT imply that an OpenZeppelin contract audit covers Smart Account Kit, the relayer, indexer, frontend, policies, and deployment configuration as one inherited guarantee.
Do NOT confuse transaction submission or fee sponsorship with smart-account authorization, or call Smart Account Kit SDF-maintained.
golden.notes: GT-56 SAFETY/FRESHNESS CORRECTION 2026-07-11: Smart Account Kit 0.4.2 plus exact selected OpenZeppelin account contracts is the greenfield route. Passkey Kit is legacy, explicitly unaudited demo material, and warns against real-value use. Do not assume identical primitives or inherited full-stack audit coverage.

corroboration.claim: Passkey Kit is the legacy predecessor and its current repository warns that it is unaudited demo material not suitable for securing real value.

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The inherited migration direction was right, but it omitted Passkey Kit's explicit unaudited/demo/no-real-value warning, used a stale successor version, treated both kits as exposing identical primitives, and allowed OpenZeppelin audit scope to flow across the whole application stack.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-56 primary process 3394 and sealed blind process 3398 independently retained the successor direction and required the legacy safety warning, exact versioning, and audit-scope boundary.
Sibling sweep checked q-tool-passkey-wallet-recovery, q-tool-smart-wallet-repos-discovery, q-infra-secp256r1-passkeys, and q-soroban-add-signer-smart-wallet-howto.

The stale official smart-wallet guide routing is recorded separately in improvements/stellar-docs/sd-034-smart-wallet-passkey-kit-legacy-routing.md; sd-027 remains limited to LaunchTube submission/indexing migration.
rootCause: Eval-side audit-scope and API-lineage overreach: a dependency's audit and broad signer vocabulary were promoted to the entire SDK/application stack.
